dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic had the strongest Hacker News vibe on 2026-04-17: the rank-1 "Claude Design" thread was broadly impressed and saw real utility/potential in the product [E1], which outweighed separate skepticism about Claude pricing [E2] and sharp criticism of Claude on circuit-design tasks [E4].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic dominated the day's relevant discussion volume and still came out ahead overall. Its biggest story, "Claude Design," drew substantial enthusiasm and practical-use praise, with commenters calling the tech impressive and useful for speeding up mockups and communication [E1]. That positive attention was partially offset by cost skepticism in the tokenizer thread [E2] and by negative anecdotes about Claude/Opus hallucinating in electronics workflows [E4]. OpenAI only got a small positive side mention via Codex [E5], Microsoft Copilot only showed up in a pricing/usage complaint [E3], and Google Gemini had no clear standalone signal. Because the winner should reflect both positivity and amount of coverage, Anthropic wins despite a mixed overall day.

openai.judgementSnippet: Light but mildly positive signal: a commenter said they switched a hardware project to Codex after Claude failed, giving OpenAI a small favorable comparison [E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Heavy coverage and mixed-but-net-positive signal: Claude Design drew strong enthusiasm and productivity praise [E1], but Claude pricing/tokenizer discussion was skeptical [E2] and Claude Code received harsh criticism on circuit-design tasks [E4].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Limited but negative signal: GitHub Copilot was criticized for a usage-multiplier increase from 3 to 7.5, framed as Microsoft reducing subsidy/losses [E3].

evidence.summary: HN story "Claude Design." Top comments called the technology impressive and practically useful for rapidly creating mockups and communicating intent, with some saying it could be a real threat to Figma; dissent focused on sameness and lack of creativity rather than product failure.

HN story "Measuring Claude 4.7's tokenizer costs." Top comments worried Anthropic's recent gains may come with sharply higher operating costs or pricing, and questioned whether a 20-30% cost increase delivers noticeable value, though some pushed back that per-task value matters more than token price.
In the Claude tokenizer-cost thread, a commenter complained that GitHub Copilot's multiplier rose from 3 to 7.5 and sarcastically described this as Microsoft wanting to lose money more slowly.

HN story "Show HN: SPICE simulation → oscilloscope → verification with Claude Code." Multiple top comments said Claude/Opus hallucinated hardware capabilities, invented nonexistent details, and performed poorly on circuit design and optimization tasks.
In the SPICE/Claude Code thread, one commenter said Claude failed badly enough that they completed the project in Codex instead, creating a direct favorable comparison for OpenAI.
